tasks.title: Select the licensed MVP car catalog and source assets
tasks.summary: Research and document a distribution-compatible dataset, pretrained-weight baseline, and exact 100–200-class MVP catalog without downloading or committing unapproved assets.
tasks.objective: Resolve the accepted catalog and licensing gate before dataset preparation or model distribution begins.
tasks.context: The merged requirements require approximately 100–200 common car models and compatible licenses, but intentionally leave the exact classes and source assets open.
outputs: A reviewed catalog manifest with stable class IDs and display labels
A dataset and pretrained-weight license matrix with source links and redistribution conclusions
A decision record identifying accepted sources, exclusions, and unresolved acquisition risks
implementation_notes: Treat dataset images, annotations, pretrained weights, and generated model weights as separately licensed artifacts.
Do not equate public download access with permission to redistribute.
Include generation or approximate-year distinctions only where the accepted product scope requires them.
acceptance_criteria: The proposed catalog contains between 100 and 200 unique stable class IDs.
Every proposed dataset and pretrained weight has a recorded license, source, attribution requirement, and application-distribution conclusion.
Any incompatible or unclear source is excluded or explicitly marked blocking rather than treated as accepted.
The human owner records acceptance of the catalog and asset-source decision.
verification: Validate catalog uniqueness and required fields with a deterministic script or schema.
Manually review source license pages and preserve direct references and access dates.
Confirm no raw dataset, credentials, or unapproved model binary is added to Git.
proof_required: Human acceptance recorded in the decision document
edge_cases: Multiple names for the same generation
Dataset labels that collapse generations required by the MVP
non_goals: Expanding beyond the accepted 100–200 car scope
tasks.handoff_notes: ML work must consume the accepted stable class IDs and license conclusions without renaming classes silently.

tasks.title: Select the Android benchmark device and measurement protocol
tasks.summary: Choose the representative Android 10+ mid-range device and define a reproducible cold/warm inference measurement protocol.
tasks.objective: Give the accepted two-second median inference threshold a concrete hardware and measurement reference.
tasks.context: The planning package requires a selected device but intentionally leaves the exact device open.
inputs: Accepted Android 10+, recent mid-range, approximately 4 GB RAM device boundary
Accepted median inference below two seconds signal
outputs: Measurement protocol covering setup, warmup, sample count, timings, and result format
implementation_notes: Record model name, chipset, RAM, Android version, ABI, and relevant power/performance settings.
Separate image decoding/preprocessing, session initialization, and inference timing so results are interpretable.
acceptance_criteria: A physically available or otherwise guaranteed-access benchmark device is named with reproducible specifications.
The protocol defines cold and warm paths, warmup, minimum sample count, percentile/median reporting, and failure handling.
The human owner confirms access to the selected device.
verification: Review the protocol against the project acceptance signal.
Run a no-model dry run or fixture timing harness if device access is already available.
non_goals: Meeting the latency gate before a candidate model exists

tasks.title: Set the bundled application and model size budget
tasks.summary: Define accepted download, installed, model-file, and runtime-memory budgets for the closed-testing build.
tasks.objective: Bound model selection and quantization decisions with explicit release constraints.
tasks.context: The merged planning package leaves the bundled-size budget unresolved while requiring a bundled offline model.
inputs: Supported approximately 4 GB RAM device boundary
outputs: Accepted size and memory budget decision record
A measurement checklist for release artifacts and runtime memory
implementation_notes: Keep compressed download size, installed size, ONNX file size, peak resident memory, and inference working memory distinct.
Document which metric is a hard gate and which is an optimization target.
acceptance_criteria: Numeric budgets are recorded for model file, download package, installed footprint, and peak runtime memory.
The measurement method and responsible evidence location are defined.
The human owner accepts the budgets or explicitly records a blocking exception.
verification: Check the decision record contains every required metric and unit.
Validate the proposed measurement commands or Android tooling on a placeholder build when available.
non_goals: Optimizing a model before a baseline exists
Changing the accepted offline bundled-model requirement

tasks.title: Define shared car recognition and album domain contracts
tasks.summary: Create versioned Kotlin domain types and interfaces for car identities, ranked recognition results, uncertainty, album entries, and repository use cases.
tasks.objective: Give application and ML lanes a stable shared boundary before implementation starts.
tasks.context: The accepted plan defines CarIdentity, RecognitionCandidate, RecognitionResult, AlbumEntry, RecognitionEngine, and AlbumRepository responsibilities.
outputs: Documented invariants, error states, and ownership boundaries
implementation_notes: Represent uncertain and unsupported results explicitly.
Keep platform types, database entities, ONNX tensors, and UI state out of shared domain contracts.
Use stable class IDs rather than display names as identity keys.
acceptance_criteria: Every planned domain entity and required field has a typed representation or documented intentional derivation.
Recognition results preserve ordered candidates, model version, and inference duration.
Album mutations and queries cover create, update, delete, search, favorite filter, and restart-safe observation.
Contracts compile independently of Android platform and ONNX Runtime APIs.
verification: Compile the shared domain module for configured targets.
Review public interfaces for accidental platform or persistence coupling.
edge_cases: Manual identity outside the model catalog

tasks.title: Define the versioned model bundle manifest
tasks.summary: Specify and validate the model manifest, class map, preprocessing, output interpretation, hashes, ONNX compatibility, and license references.
tasks.objective: Make the training export and Android runtime consume one deterministic model contract.
tasks.context: Cross-runtime accuracy depends on exact agreement about input transforms, class order, and output ranking.
implementation_notes: Include model and class-map hashes, input tensor name/shape/type, color order, resize/crop, normalization, output tensor semantics, opset, runtime minimums, and notices.
Do not commit a real model binary as part of this contract task.
acceptance_criteria: A validator rejects missing, duplicate, reordered, or hash-inconsistent class metadata.
Preprocessing and postprocessing are unambiguous enough for independent Python and Kotlin implementations.
License and attribution references are mandatory fields for release-ready manifests.
Manifest version compatibility behavior is documented.
verification: Validate positive and negative manifest fixtures.
Round-trip the example manifest through Kotlin and Python JSON tooling when those harnesses exist.
Review the contract against ONNX Runtime input/output metadata needs.
non_goals: Training, quantizing, or benchmarking the model

tasks.title: Define the versioned album archive contract
tasks.summary: Specify a checksummed, privacy-preserving export/import archive with validation, conflict rules, and atomic failure semantics.
tasks.objective: Allow local backup and transfer without coupling the durable format to internal database details.
tasks.context: Manual export/import is an accepted MVP requirement and must be suitable for later migration or synchronization.
implementation_notes: Use logical entry IDs and relative archive paths; never persist device-absolute file paths.
Validate all metadata, paths, sizes, and checksums before mutating local state.
Exclude unnecessary original image metadata from exported managed copies.
acceptance_criteria: The contract contains archive version, timestamps, entries, relative image references, and checksums.
Path traversal, missing files, checksum mismatch, incompatible versions, duplicates, and partial archives have defined outcomes.
Import validation is separable from commit so invalid archives cause no partial mutation.
The format does not expose internal database implementation details.
verification: Validate good, corrupted, missing-file, duplicate-ID, incompatible-version, and path-traversal fixtures.
Review fixtures for absence of location and unnecessary device metadata.

tasks.title: Publish cross-lane contract fixtures and fake adapters
tasks.summary: Provide deterministic recognition, album, model-manifest, and archive fixtures plus fake interfaces that unblock application, ML, and verification lanes.
tasks.objective: Let parallel consumers implement and test against the same examples before real adapters are complete.
tasks.context: The planning package explicitly allows the application lane to use a deterministic fake recognition engine.
implementation_notes: Fixtures must cover successful, uncertain, unsupported, corrupt, and incompatible cases.
Keep fixtures small and free of third-party images or unclear licenses.
acceptance_criteria: Application tests can drive all recognition result states without ONNX Runtime.
Archive and manifest consumers share identical checked-in fixtures.
Fake adapters implement the accepted interfaces without production-only behavior.
Fixture ownership and change-review rules are documented.
verification: Validate every JSON fixture against its schema.
Compile fake implementations with the shared domain module.
